Home / Media Streaming / Alternatives to Google Photos

3 open source alternatives to Google Photos

Photo backup and AI-organized albums. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Google Photos

  • Photos fed into Google's indexing.
  • Free "high quality" upload ended in 2021.
  • Export is slow.

Current Google Photos pricing (for reference): 15 GB free shared with Google account.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Immich
Open source Google Photos alternative with ML tagging. 		Modern, mobile-first photo server with ML tagging. AGPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Self-host only
PhotoPrism
AI-powered photo library you can self-host. 		Stable, mature photo library when you prefer proven software. AGPL-3.0 ★★☆☆☆ Self-host only
Nextcloud
Self-hosted Dropbox and Google Drive alternative — files, calendar, contacts and more. 		Photos alongside files, contacts and calendar in one place. AGPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes

1. Immich — Modern, mobile-first photo server with ML tagging.

Open source Google Photos alternative with ML tagging.

Strengths

  • AI face and object recognition — fully local.
  • Mobile auto-upload that actually works.
  • Fast, modern web UI.

Weaknesses

  • Project explicitly marked "not production-ready" for critical data.
  • GPU recommended for ML features.
  • Ongoing breaking changes — frequent updates.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Desktop: Windows, macOS, LinuxMobile: iOS, Android

Immich homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Photos vs Immich →

2. PhotoPrism — Stable, mature photo library when you prefer proven software.

AI-powered photo library you can self-host.

Strengths

  • Mature, stable self-host option.
  • ML tagging works on CPU (slowly) or GPU.
  • Clean UI with maps and albums.

Weaknesses

  • Mobile apps are third-party webview wrappers.
  • Some power features in sponsor-only builds.
  • Auto-upload flow is more manual than Immich.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 2/5 Mobile: iOS, Android

PhotoPrism homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Photos vs PhotoPrism →

3. Nextcloud — Photos alongside files, contacts and calendar in one place.

Self-hosted Dropbox and Google Drive alternative — files, calendar, contacts and more.

Strengths

  • Huge app ecosystem — Talk, Office, Mail, Deck.
  • Strong privacy and encryption features.
  • Works well on small home servers or enterprise deployments.

Weaknesses

  • Performance depends heavily on setup (PHP + DB tuning).
  • Many features come from third-party apps with variable quality.
  • Mobile photo sync occasionally needs manual fixes.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud optionDesktop: Windows, macOS, LinuxMobile: iOS, Android

Nextcloud homepage · Source on GitHub · Google Photos vs Nextcloud →
